{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided in the documents, alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) is primarily produced by the fetal yolk sac and liver. It is found in fetal urine and then excreted into the amniotic fluid. From the amniotic fluid, it enters the maternal circulation. Therefore, the maximum level of alpha-fetoprotein would be found in the amniotic fluid, where it is directly produced and present in high concentrations before entering the maternal serum. The other options, such as fetal serum, placenta, and maternal serum, would have lower levels of AFP.", "answer_choice": "C"}
